feed.title: ZA
feed.subtitle: Wish my blogs can give you a help!

entry.title: 9.13 Group Meeting
entry.content:
Literature Review of Text SLAM


Professor told me that he was surprised by the development of Vision-Language Models and suggested me to learn about it.

Problems I have met in ORB-SLAM3


In the process of circumventing the shelves and returning, the trajectory should rotate 360 degrees, but the trajectory generated by orb-slam3 is less than 270 degrees.
Professor suggested me to slow down when returning and avoid pure rotation.

Data connection between cpp programme and python programme via socket

ID detection is designed to be applied on keyframes. It's benefits are as follow: 1. reduce the amount to detect and ensure the system can run real-time. 2. The position of the ID can be optimized along with the optimization of the keyframe poses.
So I edit the Tracking.cc. When a new keyframe generated, the RGB image and the deep image will be sent for detection via socket.
The disadvantage of such method is that the ID detection is still not dived from Tracking thread. I'm going to make ID a seperate thread just like Loop CLosure and Local Mapping.

Professor suggested me to try ROS2.

To Do

  1. Learn about Vision-Language Models and think about how to apply it into the project.
  2. Design and code the ID thread.

entry.title: Note | Vision-Language Modeling
entry.content:
An Introduction to Vision-Language Modeling

[2405.17247] An Introduction to Vision-Language Modeling (arxiv.org)

Abstract

Challenges needed to be addressed.

Language is discrete, vision is in a high dimensional space in which concepts cannot always be easily discretized.

Introduction

Problems unsolved in connecting language to vision:

  1. Struggle to understand spatial relationships(Fei-fei Li is focusing on spatial intelligence) or count without complicated engineering overhead that relies on additional data annotation.
  2. Lack an understanding of attributes and ordering.
  3. Hallucinate and produce content that is neither required nor relevant.

The Families of VLMs

Four different training paradigms:

  1. Contrastive training: leverages pairs of positive and negative examples.
  2. Masking: leverages reconstruction of masked image patched given some unmasked text.
  3. Pretrained backbones: leverage open-source LLMs like Llama to learn a mapping between an image encoder and the LLM.
  4. Generative.

Many approaches rely on a mix of contrastive, masking and generative criteria.

Early work on VLMs based on transformers

visual-BERT and ViL-BERT: combine text with images tokens

The models are trained on two objectives: 1) a classical masked modelling task that aims to predict the missing part in a given input; and 2) a sentence-image prediction task that aims to predict if a caption is actually describing an image content.

Contrastive-based VLMs

Energy-Based Models(EBM) can better explain contrastive-based trainning.

Data from a target distribution should have low energy while any other data points should have higher energy.

Consider input data xxx with an energy function Eθ(x)E_\theta (x)Eθ​(x) of parameters θ\thetaθ. The corresponding Boltzman distribution density function to learn:

Zθ=∑xe−Eθ(x)Z_\theta=\sum_x e^{-E_\theta(x)}Zθ​=∑x​e−Eθ​(x) is the normalization factor.

Use the traditional maximum likelihood objective to estimate the target distribution PDP_DPD​

To find the θ\thetaθ, we need to calculate the Gradient:

The above requires x−∼Pθ(x)x^- \sim P_\theta(x)x−∼Pθ​(x), which corresponds to a sample from the model distribution that can be intractable (ZθZ_\thetaZθ​ is difficult to calculate). There are several techniques to approximate such a distribution.

  1. Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC): find examples that minimize the predicted energy through an iterative process.
  2. Score Matching and Denoising Score Matching criteria: remove the normalization factor by learning only the gradient of the probability density with respect to the input data.
  3. Noise Contrastive Estimation(NCE).

NCE: Sampling from a noise distribution u′∼pn(u′)u' \sim p_n(u')u′∼pn​(u′) might approximate samples from the model distribution well enough in certain instances.

InfoNCE:

Instead of predicting a binary value, the InfoNCE loss leverages a distance metric, such as cosine similarity, computed in a model representation space.

CLIP

Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP): a common contrastive method using the InfoNCE loss.

The positive pairs of examples are defined as one image and its corresponding ground truth caption while the negative examples are defined as the same image but with all the other captions contained in the mini-batch that described the other images.

Incorporate vision and language in a shared representation of an image and its caption to similar embedding vectors using a contrastive loss.

SigLIP: similar to CLIP but it uses the original NCE loss.

Llip: It proposes to condition the encoding of an image on the target caption via a cross-attention module.

VLMs with masking objectives

Masking can be viewed as a specific form of denoising autoencoder in which the noise has spatial structure.

FLAVA

Foundational Language And Vision Alignment (FLAVA)

Three core components:

  1. The Image Encoder: Vision Transformer(ViT) process images into patches for linear embedding and transformer-based representation, including a classification token ([CLSI]).
  2. The Text Encoder: tokenizes textual input using a transformer [Vaswani et al., 2017] and embeds them into vectors for contextual processing and outputting hidden state vectors alongside a classification token ([CLST]).
  3. The multimodal Encoder: fuses hidden states from both the image and text encoders, leveraging learned linear projections and cross-attention mechanisms within the transformer framework to integrate visual and textual information, highlighted by an additional multimodal classification token ([CLSM]).

MaskVLM

Information theoretic view on VLM objectives

Generative-based VLMs

In contrast to previous training paradigms which mostly operate on latent representations to build images or text abstractions that are then mapped between each other, the generative paradigm considers the generation of text and/or images.

CoCa

An example of learning a text generator

Employ the contrastive loss and a generative loss.

Chameleon and CM3leon

CM3leon: two-stage training process.

  1. retrieval-augmented pretraining
  2. involves supervised fine-tuning (SFT), where the model undergoes multi-task instruction tuning.

Chameleon is an extension to CM3leon.

VLMs from Pretrained Backbones

Frozen

first example of a model leveraging a pretrained LLM.

Others

MiniGPT, Qwen, BLIP2

entry.title: Note | ORB-SLAM3 source code(1)
entry.content:
It's the beginning of a series, which is a note taken while reading the source code of orb-slam3. The original version from UZ-SLAMLab has some trouble when running with ROS. So I adapt another version supporting ROS whose link is https://github.com/thien94/orb_slam3_ros.

In this note, I am going to introduce ros_rgbd.cc , system.cc and ORBextractor.cc

Some important things

The order of naming

The first(or with second) letter of a variable should obey the following rules:

  • m for member variable of a class;

  • p for pointer;

  • n for int;

  • b for bool;

  • s for std::set;

  • v for std::vector;

  • l for std::list;

  • KF for KeyFrame.

Multithreading

Three threads: Tracking, Local Mapping, Loop Closing.

Only the selected frame, that is, keyframes, can enter local mapping and loop closing.

Because of the existence of Multithreading, we need to use mutex to avoid chaos. The member variables are set private or protected, and the addition of mutex is in the public functions operating them. For example,

class KeyFrame{

protected:

    KeyFrame* mpParent;

    ...

public:

    KeyFrame* KeyFrame::GetParent()

    {

        unique_lock<mutex> lockCon(mMutexConnections);

        return mpParent;

    }

}

ros_rgbd.cc

An ImageGrabber class with a method GrabRGBD is defined in this file.

class ImageGrabber

{

public:

​    ImageGrabber(){};

​    void GrabRGBD(const sensor_msgs::ImageConstPtr& msgRGB,const sensor_msgs::ImageConstPtr& msgD);

};

In the Main Function:

  • Some preparing jobs, such as initialzing ROS and loading vocabulary file.

  • Initialization of the SLAM system.

 pSLAM = new ORB_SLAM3::System(voc_file, settings_file, sensor_type, enable_pangolin);

  • Creates an instance of ImageGrabber

  • Sets up subscribers for RGB and depth images and synchronizes them using an approximate time policy.

  • Registers a callback function that calls GrabRGBD.

  • Enters the ROS event loop with ros::spin(), which will process callbacks until the node is shut down.

  • Shuts down the SLAM system and ROS when the node is shut down.

ImageGrabber::GrabRGBD Method:

  • Converts ROS images messages to OpenCV cv::Mat format using cv_bridge.

  • Call the TrackRGBD method of the SLAM system to process the RGBD images and obtain camera pose.

The System class

Some important member functions and member variables:

Functions/Members Access Modifiers Meaning
Sophus::SE3f TrackRGBD(const cv::Mat &im, const cv::Mat &depthmap, const double &timestamp, const vectorIMU::Point& vImuMeas = vectorIMU::Point(), string filename=""); public Process the given rgbd frame. Depthmap must be registered to the RGB frame.
System(const string &strVocFile, const string &strSettingsFile, const eSensor sensor, const bool bUseViewer = true, const int initFr = 0, const string &strSequence = std::string()); pubic Initialize the SLAM system. It launches the Local Mapping, Loop Closing and Viewer threads.
eSensor mSensor private Input sensor
Tracking* mpTracker private It receives a frame and computes the associated camera pose. It also decides when to insert a new keyframe, create some new MapPoints and performs relocalization if tracking fails.
LocalMapping* mpLocalMapper private
std::thread* mptLocalMapping private
LoopClosing* mpLoopCloser private
std::thread* mptLoopClosing private

Constructor

Some parts of the code have been omitted.

System::System(const string &strVocFile, const string &strSettingsFile, const eSensor sensor,
               const bool bUseViewer, const int initFr, const string &strSequence):
    mSensor(sensor), mpViewer(static_cast<Viewer*>(NULL)), mbReset(false), mbResetActiveMap(false),
    mbActivateLocalizationMode(false), mbDeactivateLocalizationMode(false), mbShutDown(false)
{
    // Output welcome message
    //...

    //Check settings file
    cv::FileStorage fsSettings(strSettingsFile.c_str(), cv::FileStorage::READ);
    //...
    
    //Load ORB Vocabulary
    //...

    //Create KeyFrame Database
    mpKeyFrameDatabase = new KeyFrameDatabase(*mpVocabulary);

    bool loadedAtlas = false;
    if(mStrLoadAtlasFromFile.empty())
    {
        //Create the Atlas
        cout << "Initialization of Atlas from scratch " << endl;
        mpAtlas = new Atlas(0);
    }
    else
    {//...

    //Create Drawers. These are used by the Viewer
    mpFrameDrawer = new FrameDrawer(mpAtlas);
    mpMapDrawer = new MapDrawer(mpAtlas, strSettingsFile, settings_);

    //Initialize the Tracking thread
    //(it will live in the main thread of execution, the one that called this constructor)
    cout << "Seq. Name: " << strSequence << endl;
    mpTracker = new Tracking(this, mpVocabulary, mpFrameDrawer, mpMapDrawer,
                             mpAtlas, mpKeyFrameDatabase, strSettingsFile, mSensor, settings_, strSequence);

    //Initialize the Local Mapping thread and launch
    mpLocalMapper = new LocalMapping(this, mpAtlas, mSensor==MONOCULAR || mSensor==IMU_MONOCULAR,
                                     mSensor==IMU_MONOCULAR || mSensor==IMU_STEREO || mSensor==IMU_RGBD, strSequence);
    mptLocalMapping = new thread(&ORB_SLAM3::LocalMapping::Run,mpLocalMapper);
    //...

    //Initialize the Loop Closing thread and launch
    mpLoopCloser = new LoopClosing(mpAtlas, mpKeyFrameDatabase, mpVocabulary, mSensor!=MONOCULAR, activeLC); // mSensor!=MONOCULAR);
    mptLoopClosing = new thread(&ORB_SLAM3::LoopClosing::Run, mpLoopCloser);

    //Set pointers between threads
    mpTracker->SetLocalMapper(mpLocalMapper);
    mpTracker->SetLoopClosing(mpLoopCloser);

    mpLocalMapper->SetTracker(mpTracker);
    mpLocalMapper->SetLoopCloser(mpLoopCloser);

    mpLoopCloser->SetTracker(mpTracker);
    mpLoopCloser->SetLocalMapper(mpLocalMapper);

    //Initialize the Viewer thread and launch
    if(bUseViewer)
    {
        mpViewer = new Viewer(this, mpFrameDrawer,mpMapDrawer,mpTracker,strSettingsFile,settings_);
        mptViewer = new thread(&Viewer::Run, mpViewer);
        mpTracker->SetViewer(mpViewer);
        mpLoopCloser->mpViewer = mpViewer;
        mpViewer->both = mpFrameDrawer->both;
    }

    // Fix verbosity
    Verbose::SetTh(Verbose::VERBOSITY_QUIET);

}

As LocalMapping and LoopClosing threads have corresponding std::thread type member variables, why the Tracking thread doesn't have corresponding std::thread type member variable?

Because the Tracking thread is the main thread. The LocalMapping and LoopClosing thread is the son threads, controlled by the main thread by two pointers(mpLocalMapping and mpLoopClosing).

TrackRGBD

Sophus::SE3f System::TrackRGBD(const cv::Mat &im, const cv::Mat &depthmap, const double &timestamp, const vector<IMU::Point>& vImuMeas, string filename)
{
    // Check if the type of sensor is right. Clone and resize the images.
    //...

    // Check mode change
    //...

    // Check reset
    //...

    if (mSensor == System::IMU_RGBD)
        for(size_t i_imu = 0; i_imu < vImuMeas.size(); i_imu++)
            mpTracker->GrabImuData(vImuMeas[i_imu]);

    // Use the Tracker to track
    Sophus::SE3f Tcw = mpTracker->GrabImageRGBD(imToFeed,imDepthToFeed,timestamp,filename);
	
    // Update system status
    unique_lock<mutex> lock2(mMutexState);
    mTrackingState = mpTracker->mState;
    mTrackedMapPoints = mpTracker->mCurrentFrame.mvpMapPoints;
    mTrackedKeyPointsUn = mpTracker->mCurrentFrame.mvKeysUn;
    mTrackedKeyPoints = mpTracker->mCurrentFrame.mvKeys;

    return Tcw;
}

The ORBextractor class

Some important points:

  1. Pyramid
  2. Distribute feature points evenly: (1) Divide into cells, and if the response values of feature points in a certain cell are generally small, lower the threshold and search again. (2) If feature points are densely in a certain cell, only select the points with higher response values. (vector<cv::KeyPoint> ORBextractor::DistributeOctTree(const vector<cv::KeyPoint>& vToDistributeKeys, const int &minX, const int &maxX, const int &minY, const int &maxY, const int &N, const int &level))

The ORBextractor class is used through the function int ORBextractor::operator()( InputArray _image, InputArray _mask, vector<KeyPoint>& _keypoints, OutputArray _descriptors, std::vector<int> &vLappingArea)

In the Frame class, the ORBextractor class is used in the function ExtractORB()

void Frame::ExtractORB(int flag, const cv::Mat &im, const int x0, const int x1)
{
    vector<int> vLapping = {x0,x1};
    if(flag==0)
        monoLeft = (*mpORBextractorLeft)(im,cv::Mat(),mvKeys,mDescriptors,vLapping);
    else
        monoRight = (*mpORBextractorRight)(im,cv::Mat(),mvKeysRight,mDescriptorsRight,vLapping);
}

entry.title: Record | D435i to gain the depth of objects
entry.content:
A Chinese version is in https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/715385774

Environment: Ubuntu 20.04.6 LTS, ROS2 foxy, python 3.8

Install RealSense SDK and realsense-viewer

Follow the instructions of the official guide: https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blob/master/doc/distribution_linux.md

The Error

I met an error when enter sudo apt-get install librealsense2-utils.

shengzi@shengzi:~$ sudo apt-get install librealsense2-utils
正在读取软件包列表... 完成
正在分析软件包的依赖关系树
正在读取状态信息... 完成
有一些软件包无法被安装。如果您用的是 unstable 发行版，这也许是因为系统无法达到您要求的状态造成的。该版本中可能会有一些您需要的软件包尚未被创建或是它们已被从新到(Incoming)目录移出。
下列信息可能会对解决问题有所帮助：
下列软件包有未满足的依赖关系：
librealsense2-utils : 依赖: libgtk-3-dev 但是它将不会被安装
E: 无法修正错误，因为您要求某些软件包保持现状，就是它们破坏了软件包间的依赖关系。

And when I try to install libgtk-3-dev, an another error occurred.

shengzi@shengzi:~$ sudo apt-get install libgtk-3-dev
正在读取软件包列表... 完成
正在分析软件包的依赖关系树
正在读取状态信息... 完成
有一些软件包无法被安装。如果您用的是 unstable 发行版，这也许是因为系统无法达到您要求的状态造成的。该版本中可能会有一些您需要的软件包尚未被创建或是它们已被从新到(Incoming)目录移出。
下列信息可能会对解决问题有所帮助：

下列软件包有未满足的依赖关系：
libgtk-3-dev : 依赖: libatk-bridge2.0-dev 但是它将不会被安装
E: 无法修正错误，因为您要求某些软件包保持现状，就是它们破坏了软件包间的依赖关系。

Refer to https://stackoverflow.com/questions/66375573/install-libgtk-3-dev-on-ubuntu-20-04, the error can be solved as follow:

  1. Open and edit sources.list.
sudo gedit /etc/apt/sources.list
  1. Add following content and save: deb Index of /ubuntu focal-updates main.
  2. Update and install again.
sudo apt update
sudo apt-get install libgtk-3-dev

The Result

You can open RealSense Viewer to check images stream after installation.

realsense-viewer

Install realsense-ros

Follow the instructions in https://github.com/IntelRealSense/realsense-ros.

The Error

In the step rosdep install -i --from-path src --rosdistro $ROS_DISTRO --skip-keys=librealsense2 -y, two libraries are required: xacro and diagnostic_updater. Enter following commands in terminal to install them.

sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-xacro
sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-diagnostic-updater

The Result

Run ros2 run rviz2 rviz2 to start rviz and selet corresponding topics to view the message that D435i sends via ROS.

Detect the objects and gain their depth

Detect

Install YOLOv8 following https://github.com/ultralytics/ultralytics

Estimate the Depth

Some points need to be emphasised:

  1. Remember to align the color and depth.
  2. Remember to use depth_scale to change the unit.
    Considering that there are pixels which don't belong to objects, I utilize RANSAC algorithm to estimate the depth of objects.
def estimate_depth_with_ransac(depth_data, num_samples=50):
    # randomly select samples
    sampled_depths = np.random.choice(depth_data.flatten(), num_samples, replace=False)
    # estimate depth using RANSAC
    ransac = RANSACRegressor()
    X = np.arange(len(sampled_depths)).reshape(-1, 1)
    ransac.fit(X, sampled_depths)
    inlier_mask = ransac.inlier_mask_
    estimated_depth = np.mean(sampled_depths[inlier_mask])
    return estimated_depth

The whole program can be obtained from my github repository: https://github.com/zhuangaoooo/Tools-of-D435i.

The Result

entry.title: Survey | YOLO在SLAM中的应用
entry.content:
最近想在SLAM中加入YOLO，于是先看看同行做了哪些工作。

处理动态场景

用YOLO来处理动态场景的思路很简单：检测到那些会动的目标（如人、车），并把相关区域内的点移除，不让这些点进入SLAM中。因此这些文章的不同之处就在于两个方面：如何检测目标，如何选取移除的区域。

Visual SLAM in Human Populated Environments: Exploring the Trade-off between Accuracy and Speed of YOLO and Mask R-CNN
Soares, Joao Carlos Virgolino, Marcelo Gattass, and Marco Antonio Meggiolaro. "Visual SLAM in human populated environments: exploring the trade-off between accuracy and speed of YOLO and Mask R-CNN." 2019 19th International Conference on Advanced Robotics (ICAR). IEEE, 2019.
这篇文章只考虑了人作为动态物体。作者做了两个尝试，一个是用Mask R-CNN进行像素级别的实例分割，然后将属于人的键点移除；另一个则是用YOLO进行目标检测，然后将检测方框内的点全部移除。两种方法都是基于ORB-SLAM2的，其中建图线程和回环线程与ORB-SLAM2相同。
前者的资源消耗以及时间消耗是很大的，远不能满足实时性的要求。后者更快更轻量级，但准确率有下降。不过不得不吐槽，作者直接把检测方框内的所有特征点都移除实在有点简单粗暴了。

YOLO-SLAM: A semantic SLAM system towards dynamic environment with geometric constraint
Wu, Wenxin, et al. "YOLO-SLAM: A semantic SLAM system towards dynamic environment with geometric constraint." Neural Computing and Applications (2022): 1-16.
核心思想与上一篇文章类似：阻止动态物体上的特征点进入SLAM，但这篇文章更加细致深入一些。
在YOLO识别到动态目标之后，作者用随机样本一致性RANSAC通过深度差异在方框中区分动态与静态，并去除动态范围内的点（前一篇文章是不论动态和静态把检测方框内的点全部去掉）。

随机样本一致性（RANSAC, Random Sample Consensus）可以在含大噪声的数据中估计参数，是一种跌代方法。作者使用这种方法基于三个假设：1.人是最有可能移动的物体，占据了大部分边界框。2.出现在人身上的特征点的深度值差别不大。3.在边界框中，人与周围物体的深度差异明显。
文章中给出了具体Depth-RANSAC method的伪代码：

回环检测

Loop Closure Detection for Visual SLAM Systems Based on Convolutional Netural Network
Shi, Xiangbin, and Lin Li. "Loop closure detection for visual SLAM systems based on convolutional netural network." 2021 IEEE 24th International Conference on Computational Science and Engineering (CSE). IEEE, 2021.
传统回环检测是利用词袋模型(BoW, Bag-of-Words model)，词袋模型本质上也是用图像上有哪几种特征来描述一幅图像。这篇文章所做的就是用YOLO目标检测出来的向量来代替词袋模型里面的词。
说白了就是：用YOLOv4进行目标检测，利用LSH算法把相似的图像（根据目标检测输出的向量来判断）放进一个bucket中以避免暴力匹配，最后在bucket中根据余弦距离计算相似度。

语义SLAM

Semantic SLAM Based on Object Detection and Improved Octomap
Zhang, Liang, et al. "Semantic SLAM based on object detection and improved octomap." IEEE Access 6 (2018): 75545-75559.
这篇文章值得关注的内容其实挺多的：

  1. 用YOLO生成语义信息后，如何构建关键帧与物体之间的关系。
  2. 修改ORB-SLAM2中的Tracking线程以降低动态物体的影响，减少因相机运动过快而跟丢的情况，生成更多关键帧。
  3. 考虑对象之间的语义上下文关系来优化和纠正由YOLO算法检测到的每个对象的类别标签。
  4. 利用Fast Line Rasterization算法和多线程来提升八叉树地图的效率并加快建图速度。
    这篇文章实验做得也很详实，里面的很多思路也值得参考，改天应该会再写一篇博客详细谈一谈这篇文章的具体内容。

总结

目标检测在最近几年发展非常迅速，在精度和效率上都有很大提升，其中的YOLO更是能达到实时性要求。不过，目前只有零星文章把YOLO应用在SLAM中，并且其中没有特别突出的作品。个人认为这个方向还有很大的挖掘空间。
